steps.title: Check Table A
steps.content:
For each pair in table A, substitute $x$ and check if the resulting inequality holds true.
Start with (3, 20):\[y < 6 \cdot 3 + 2\]\[20 < 18 + 2\]$$20 < 20$$ does not hold true.

steps.title: Check Table B
steps.content:
Move to table B and perform the same check.
First row: (3, 16):\[16 < 6 \cdot 3 + 2\]\[16 < 18 + 2\]$$16 < 20$$ is true.
Second row: (5, 36):\[36 ? 6 \cdot 5 + 2\] $$36 > 32 $$ does not hold.

steps.title: Check Table C
steps.content:
• Verify table C.
    First row: (3, 16):\[16 < 6 \cdot 3 + 2\]\[16 < 20$$ holds.
  • Second row: (5, 28):$$28 < 50 + 2$$ $$28 < 32$$. True.
  • Final row: (7, 40):\[y<6 \cdot 7+2\]$$40 < 42$$. Holds true.
    All pairs satisfy the inequality in table C.
steps.formula: $$y < mx+b$$ where $m = 6, b = 2$, and $(x,y)$ are the pairs from Table C.
